basic_details.job_title: Punjab Lecturer Cadre Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 1013 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Directorate of School Education, Recruitment Directorate, Punjab has officially released the Punjab Lecturer Recruitment 2026 notification for 1,013 vacancies (829 fresh + 184 backlog) across 12 subjects including Punjabi, English, History, Mathematics, and more. Candidates can apply online from 1 July 2026 to 31 July 2026 at erd.punjab.gov.in. Eligibility requires an M.A. with at least 55% marks and B.Ed in the concerned subject. The application fee is ₹2,000 for General and ₹1,000 for SC/BC candidates. Physically Handicapped applicants are exempt from fee.

application_fee.details: The application fee for Punjab Lecturer Recruitment 2026 is ₹2,000 for General category candidates and ₹1,000 for SC/BC/ST category candidates. Physically Handicapped (PH) candidates are fully exempt from the application fee. The fee must be paid online through the official portal at the time of registration.

age_limit.details: Candidates wondering about the age limit for Punjab Lecturer Vacancy 2026 can note that the minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 37 years as on 1 January 2026. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved category candidates as per Punjab government rules. SC/BC candidates of Punjab get upper age relaxation. Ex-servicemen, physically handicapped, and other special category candidates are also entitled to relaxation as per applicable government orders. | Category Age Limit General 18 to 37 years (as on 01.01.2026) SC / BC / Physically Handicapped Relaxation as per Punjab Govt. rules Ex-Servicemen Relaxation as per Punjab Govt. rules

qualification.details: Candidates wondering about the educational qualification for Punjab Lecturer Cadre Recruitment 2026 must have passed M.A. in the concerned subject with at least 55% marks and B.Ed with the teaching subject from a recognised university or institution as per UGC guidelines. They must also have studied the relevant subject as an elective in Graduation for a period of three years. SC/ST/OBC/BC and Physically Handicapped candidates are eligible with 50% marks in M.A. or Post-Graduation. | For each subject post, the candidate must have: | M.A. in the concerned subject with a minimum of 55% marks (50% for reserved categories) from a recognised university, and B.Ed with the teaching subject from a recognised university/institution as per UGC guidelines, and Should have studied the relevant subject as an elective in Graduation for a period of three years. | Or any other equivalent qualification, provided a certificate of equivalency is issued by the recognised university as per UGC guidelines. | Candidates wondering about the age limit for Punjab Lecturer Vacancy 2026 can note that the minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 37 years as on 1 January 2026. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved category candidates as per Punjab government rules. SC/BC candidates of Punjab get upper age relaxation. Ex-servicemen, physically handicapped, and other special category candidates are also entitled to relaxation as per applicable government orders.

vacancy_details.Information: Directorate of School Education, Recruitment Directorate, Punjab

additional_breakdown.information: Directorate of School Education, Recruitment Directorate, Punjab

selection_process: The selection process for Punjab Lecturer Cadre Recruitment 2026 consists of two stages: a written examination followed by a personal interview. Candidates who clear the written test will be shortlisted for the interview round. Final merit is determined based on the combined performance in both stages as per rules under the Punjab Educational Service (School and Inspection) Group-B Rules

Stage 1 - Written Examination: The written exam is Objective Multiple Choice type. The paper carries 150 questions for 150 marks. Questions will cover the subject-specific content along with general awareness and teaching aptitude components as specified in the syllabus.
Stage 2 - Interview: Candidates who qualify in the written examination will be called for an interview. The interview assesses subject knowledge
and suitability for the teaching role.
Final Selection: Based on the combined merit of the written exam and interview
subject to document verification and medical fitness.
how_to_apply: The Punjab Lecturer Apply Online 2026 process will begin on 1 July 2026 and close on 31 July 2026 at the official recruitment portal erd.punjab.gov.in. Candidates must complete New Registration before filling in the application form. The steps to apply online for Punjab Lecturer Cadre 2026 are given below.
Click on "New Registration" and create your account using your basic details.
Log in with your registered credentials and select the Punjab Lecturer 2026 recruitment.
Fill in the online application form with personal, educational, and category details.

Upload your photograph (1.5 × 2 inches, 80% face visible including ears) and signature (4 × 6 inches, 80% face visible including ears) in the prescribed format with a plain background. Glasses and caps are not permitted in the photo.
Pay the application fee online through the available payment gateway.
Review the filled form thoroughly, submit it, and take a printout of the confirmation for future reference.
The submitted application can be updated or edited before the last date, but no changes will be accepted after 31 July 2026.
Note: Candidates must monitor erd.punjab.gov.in regularly for any updates or corrigenda issued after the notification date.

faq.question: Q1. Has Punjab officially released the Lecturer Cadre Recruitment Notification 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, the Directorate of School Education, Recruitment Directorate, Punjab officially released the Punjab Lecturer Cadre Recruitment 2026 notification on 25 June 2026 for 1,013 vacancies across 12 subjects.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for Punjab Lecturer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online for Punjab Lecturer Vacancy 2026 is 31 July 2026. The application window opens on 1 July 2026 at erd.punjab.gov.in.

faq.question: Q3. How many total vacancies are available under Punjab Lecturer Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 1,013 vacancies are available — 829 are fresh posts and 184 are backlog posts — spread across 12 subjects including Punjabi, English, Hindi, History, Mathematics, and Sciences.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for Punjab Lecturer Cadre 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed M.A. in the concerned subject with at least 55% marks and B.Ed with the teaching subject from a recognised university. They must also have studied the subject as an elective in Graduation for three years. Reserved category candidates are eligible with 50% marks in M.A.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for Punjab Lecturer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹2,000 for General category, ₹1,000 for SC/BC candidates, and nil (no fee) for Physically Handicapped candidates.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for Punjab Lecturer Cadre Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 37 years as on 1 January 2026. Reserved category candidates are entitled to age relaxation as per applicable Punjab government rules.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for Punjab Lecturer Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of two stages: an Objective Multiple Choice written examination (150 questions, 150 marks) followed by a personal interview. Final merit is prepared based on combined performance in both stages.

faq.question: Q8. Which subject has the most vacancies in Punjab Lecturer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: History has the highest number of vacancies with 210 posts (200 fresh + 10 backlog), followed by English with 141 posts and Political Science with 131 posts.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I apply online for Punjab Lecturer Cadre Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online at the official portal erd.punjab.gov.in from 1 July 2026 onwards. The apply online link will be activated on the start date.

faq.question: Q10. Is there any EWS reservation in Punjab Lecturer Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, Economically Weaker Section (EWS) candidates are covered under the reservation policy. EWS candidates must possess a valid EWS certificate for the financial year 2025-26 as per the instructions in the official notification.
